description_long: This object consists of a string of colorful triangular pennant flags suspended between two vertical wooden posts. The posts are capped with metallic or plastic tops, and a taut cord or wire runs horizontally between them, supporting the pennants. The pennants themselves are made of fabric or lightweight plastic, each in a different color, and are evenly spaced along the cord. The flags hang downward in a point-down orientation, creating a festive and decorative appearance. The posts appear sturdy and are likely intended to be staked into the ground for stability. This type of decoration is commonly used for outdoor events, street fairs, or park celebrations.
description: A string of colorful triangular pennant flags hangs between two capped wooden posts, creating a festive decorative barrier. The flags are evenly spaced and suspended on a cord.
description_view_0: The pennant is seen at an angle, showing the depth between the two posts and the colorful flags hanging in a line.
description_view_1: A direct side view reveals the full spread of the pennant flags, clearly displaying their colors and the even spacing along the cord.
description_view_2: This angled view from the opposite side highlights the alignment of the posts and the flags, with the pennants' point-down shape visible.
description_view_3: A straight-on view from the other side, showing the flags' colors and arrangement, with both posts and the connecting cord clearly visible.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a series of solid-colored, triangular flags in red, blue, green, and purple, with no visible text or symbols. The flags are evenly spaced and hang point-down from a horizontal cord.
